qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Theme static content allows stored XSS on the storefront home page
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a theme-customization stored XSS in `POST /admin/settings/themes/edit/{id}` and storefront rendering, while the advisory describes SSTI in low-privilege-controlled first and last name fields used in checkout/address/order flows.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The session's only qualifying finding is a stored XSS in theme static-content editing and rendering, but the target advisory is an SSTI in customer-controlled checkout/address name fields that are later evaluated in order or address views.
evidence: The advisory JSON says a normal customer can inject `{{7*7}}` during checkout or address creation and the value is later evaluated in admin order and customer address views.
Bagisto v2.3.10 release notes say: "Fixed a Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) vulnerability in the first and last name fields that could be exploited by low-privileged users."
The qualifying finding report instead describes `POST /admin/settings/themes/edit/{id}` storing active HTML/CSS that is rendered with `v-html` and raw Blade output on the storefront home page.
